title: centaurium erythraea var. sublitorale
intro: Centaurium erythraea var. sublitorale, commonly known as small-flowered centaury or marsh centaury, is a perennial plant that belongs to the gentian family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its extracts. The health benefits of Centaurium erythraea var. sublitorale include its potential to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Centaurium erythraea var. sublitorale, including tinctures and infusions, are often used to support overall health and well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
